tickerText: [TENNIS] Bad Homburg quarterfinals: Emma Navarro stuns top seed Iga Swiatek 7-5, 2-6, 6-3 for her second straight win over the defending Wimbledon champion; Elina Svitolina withdraws ahead of her quarterfinal with a right hip injury; Karolina Muchova and Clara Tauson set up a quarterfinal clash; Wimbledon main draw ceremony Friday June 26 as Serena Williams awaits her first-round opponent • [SQUASH] PSA tour enters summer break after Paris finals; Challenger circuit rolls on — Benjamin Aubert wins the 15k Vendee Open on home soil in Challans with five straight upsets; Alejandro Enriquez lifts the Guatemala Open title in front of home fans, defeating top seed Ronal Palomino • [BADMINTON] YONEX US Open Super 300 hits Round of 16 at Titan Gym in Fullerton: Lee Zii Jia opens with a 21-15, 21-17 win over Nguyen Hai Dang; defending champion Beiwen Zhang returns after three months out; top seeds Presley Smith and Jennie Gai headline home mixed doubles • [PICKLEBALL] Pickleball Inc. closes a $225M Apollo-led round valuing the group at $750M; Portland PPA Challenger opens Friday at The Picklr Westbrook in Maine; Beijing Open recap — Jack Wong Hong-kit and Sahra Dennehy crowned on Championship Sunday • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 opens Friday at Ontario Convention Center outside Los Angeles with a $1.55M prize pool; defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Kanak Jha, and Lily Zhang as qualifying begins across an 11-day Grand Smash run through July 5

newsItems.title: Navarro Ousts Swiatek to Reach Bad Homburg Quarterfinals
newsItems.excerpt: Emma Navarro made it two straight wins over Iga Swiatek on Wednesday, rallying past the top seed 7-5, 2-6, 6-3 in 2 hours and 5 minutes to reach her fourth consecutive Bad Homburg quarterfinal. Navarro saved all three break points in the decider and will face Elena-Gabriela Ruse for a semifinal berth. Elina Svitolina withdrew ahead of her quarterfinal against Wang Xinyu with a right hip injury after her three-set win over Liudmila Samsonova on Tuesday.

newsItems.title: Aubert and Enriquez Headline PSA Challenger Wins
newsItems.excerpt: With the 2025/26 PSA Squash Tour season closed after the Paris finals, the Challenger circuit delivered fresh titles. French wildcard Benjamin Aubert won the 15k Vendee Open in Challans with five consecutive upset victories, including a final win over Mohamed Sharaf for the biggest title of his career. Guatemala's Alejandro Enriquez captured the 2nd Guatemala Open at home, defeating Colombian top seed Ronal Palomino 11-3, 5-11, 11-4, 11-7 for his first Challenger silverware since 2024.

newsItems.title: US Open Super 300 Reaches Round of 16 in Fullerton
newsItems.excerpt: The YONEX US Open 2026 moved into the Round of 16 on Thursday at Titan Gym in Fullerton, California. Lee Zii Jia opened his campaign with a 21-15, 21-17 win over Vietnam's Nguyen Hai Dang, while Cheam June Wei also advanced. Defending champion Beiwen Zhang is back after a three-month injury layoff, and top seed Chou Tien Chen headlines the men's draw. Mixed doubles top seeds Presley Smith and Jennie Gai lead a strong American contingent chasing a first home title in the category since 2009.

newsItems.title: United States Smash Brings $1.55M Grand Slam to Los Angeles
newsItems.excerpt: The WTT United States Smash 2026 opens Friday at the Ontario Convention Center in Greater Los Angeles, the second Grand Smash of the season with a $1.55 million prize pool. Defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, and Hugo Calderano. Americans Kanak Jha, Lily Zhang, and Amy Wang join wild cards Nandan Naresh and Sally Moyland as qualifying kicks off ahead of an 11-day run through July 5.
